---
title: "Showing File Names on Code Blocks in Quarto"
description: "A note on how to show file names on code blocks in Quarto."
date: "2026-01-24"
date-modified: "2026-01-24"
draft: false
lang: en
categories: [quarto]
---

::: {.callout-note}
Original Japanese version: [Quartoでコードブロックのファイル名を表示する](../../../posts/2026-01-24-code-filename/index.qmd)
:::

This is a note on how to show file names on code blocks.
You can show a file name by adding the `filename` attribute to a code block.

Write it like this:

```` markdown
```{.python filename="example.py"}
print("Hello, World!")
```
````

It is displayed as follows.

```{.python filename="example.py"}
print("Hello, World!")
```

The ```` ```{.python} ```` part means that syntax highlighting is applied, but the code is not executed.
It has the same meaning as ```` ```python ````.
However, when displaying a file name, you need to use the former form.

## References

- [HTML Code Blocks - Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/html-code.html#code-filename)
- [Markdown Basics - Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/markdown-basics.html#source-code)
